description: Research-only arm-action pitch variant registry. This is not a production ruleset.

armSlotNotes: Prioritize lower-slot lateral-run contexts in future copy.
Do not split from Sinker movement anchors until public baselines are verified.
records.notes: Official taxonomy separates Two-seam FB and Sinker, but public movement summaries may collapse them.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy currently exposes both Two-seam FB and Sinker as distinct pitch types.
Public summary surfaces may collapse two-seam and sinker into the combined SIFT bucket.

armSlotNotes: Useful for arsenal description, not current pitch-add logic.
records.notes: Track as a fastball-shape descriptor rather than a recommendation target.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes generic Fastball and Four-seam FB labels.

armSlotNotes: Treat as a fastball-slider bridge, not a new movement family.
records.notes: Matches the current classifier's weak supination signal from glove-side cutter movement.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Cutter as a distinct public pitch type.

armSlotNotes: Keep arm-slot notes copy-only until slot/variant evidence is stronger.
records.notes: Public data exposes Changeup, but not circle-change as a distinct raw label.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Changeup as a distinct public pitch type.
In-game collegiate kinematics found breaking balls more supinated than fastballs and changeups at ball release.

armSlotNotes: Do not overclaim slot fit until external evidence improves.
records.notes: Keep as an internal research variant only; current external evidence is too thin for a stronger claim.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Changeup and Splitter, but not kick change as a distinct public pitch type.

armSlotNotes: Keep as a first-class offspeed family.
Do not subtype further until forkball and splitter baselines are separated.
records.notes: Officially separable; fit should remain mixed until more variant-specific mechanics evidence is collected.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Splitter as a distinct public pitch type.
Breaking-ball kinematics differ from changeup kinematics, supporting a separate offspeed branch rather than flattening everything into breaking-ball logic.

armSlotNotes: Future branch under the Splitter family once movement baselines exist.
records.notes: Official public support exists, but there is not enough current evidence to recommend it in-engine.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Forkball as a distinct public pitch type.

armSlotNotes: Treat as a slider subtype, not as a standalone family.
Future copy can reference tighter movement and more gyro spin.
records.notes: Use official gyro-spin framing, but keep the variant anchored to Slider movement.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Slider and Gyroball labels.
Baseball Savant defines gyro spin as football-like spin where less of the spin contributes to movement.
Breaking balls show greater forearm supination than fastballs and changeups at ball release.

armSlotNotes: Future slot-aware copy should emphasize horizontal movement bands.
records.notes: Already supported by the engine; keep as a first-class family and variant.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Sweeper as a distinct public pitch type.
Breaking-ball mechanics are more supinated than fastball/changeup mechanics.

armSlotNotes: Treat as slider-curve bridge copy under current movement anchors.
records.notes: Official public label exists; use as a named bridge variant before considering any new family work.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Slurve as a distinct public pitch type.
Breaking-ball mechanics are more supinated than fastball/changeup mechanics.

armSlotNotes: Good candidate for slot-aware curveball copy.
Do not create a separate suppression target until KC vs CU baselines are verified.
records.notes: Raw taxonomy separates KC, but some summary movement surfaces collapse it into CUKC.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Knuckle Curve as a distinct public pitch type.
Public summary movement surfaces may collapse Curveball and Knuckle Curve into CUKC.
Curveball research consistently finds greater forearm supination than fastballs.

armSlotNotes: Potential future overlay target for steeper, slower curve movement bands.
records.notes: Keep under the Curveball family until variant-specific baselines are available.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Slow Curve as a distinct public pitch type.
Curveball research consistently finds greater forearm supination than fastballs.

records.notes: Official public label exists, but it does not fit the current eight-family engine cleanly.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Screwball as a distinct public pitch type.

records.notes: Better treated as a spin-property concept than a recommendation target.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Gyroball as a distinct public pitch type.
Baseball Savant defines gyro spin as football-like spin where less of the spin contributes to movement.

records.notes: Too specialized for the current recommendation system.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Knuckleball as a distinct public pitch type.

records.notes: Novelty pitch, not a recommendation target.

evidence.claim: Official MLB taxonomy exposes Eephus Pitch as a distinct public pitch type.
